segment-text: (Names of Subsequent Writers, in Order of Work Performed)
(Current Writer, date) Name (of company, if applicable)
EXT. DAIRY FARM -- DAY
A small, one-man operation. There's a house, a few outbuildings, corrals and a barn.
TITLE: VENTURA COUNTY, 45 MILES FROM LOS ANGELES
Angle on JIM CROWLEY, a hard-working dairy farmer, 50's, dressed in coveralls. He's opening a gate. DAIRY COWS come plodding through and head straight towards the milking barn. They know the routine.
Morning, Girls. Let's move along now, sun's been up for an hour.
Jim moves off and doesn't see the last cow. Her name is DELILA and she looks like all the rest, except she's walking backwards.
INT. MILKING BARN -- SAME DAY

Jim making his rounds, moving past the cows in their stalls, a quaint wooden sign over each: SWEET SUE, OL' BESS, MAGGIE MAY, etc. The cows are facing away from us, udders attached by clear plastic hoses to a complicated milking apparatus. We can hear the automated pumps shushing rhythmically, see the milk coursing through the hoses. Jim reaches Delila. She's completely still, her milk stuttering through the hose.
You okay, Delila?
Suddenly, Delila turns her head, looks at him, a malevolent glint in her eyes. She GROWLS.
EXT. DAIRY FARM -- MOMENTS LATER
Jim's wife, ELLEN, hanging clothes on a clothesline. She hears Jim yelling, then sees him stumbling out of the barn, scared cows running past him.
Oh my heavens!
EXT. MILKING BARN -- THAT NIGHT
Jim and Ellen have the door open a bit and they're peeking in. We hear Delila's rabid BELLOWING intermixed with CRASHING and CLANGING. Apparently, she's wrecking the place.
Well it's not anthrax or hoof and mouth. I've never seen anything like this in 35 years of dairy farming.
Ellen wants to say something, is too afraid.
What?
This is not natural, Jim.
Not natural? What's that supposed to mean?
Look at her eyes.
Angle on Delila, panting, glaring at them, eyes like molten orbs, a burgeoning, unnatural fury inside her.
I've only seen that look one other time. Remember my cousin, Thalia?
Aww Ellen -ELLEN Thalia was possessed, Jim. A demon took hold of that poor woman's body and drove her right out of her mind!
Cow's don't get possessed, Ellen. They're cows.

They hear a BELLOW that's more like a scream. Delila is galloping right at them, her face distorted into a snarl, ears back, teeth bared like a dog. In a panic, they slam the door shut as Delila rams into it, splintering the wood. Jim looks at Ellen, realizes she may be right. My God...
END COLD OPEN
INT. CAMRY -- DAY

JOSH KEELING at the wheel, drinking a chocolate milk with a straw. He's mid-30's, casually dressed, attractive, a regular guy. Music begins, the Talking Heads sing the arrestingly weird, "Once In A Lifetime" over... A MONTAGE Josh drives through a typical LA suburb, intercut with scenes of ordinary people doing ordinary things; walking their dogs, playing tennis, having coffee at Starbucks, talking on their cells. The scenes are so ordinary they're almost surreal.

Somebody once said, the only normal people are the ones you don't know very well, and how many people do you know that well? I mean really. Your family, your friends, the people you see everyday? Oh sure, they look normal, but believe me when I tell you, normal can disguise the weirdest stuff you can imagine.
The montage ends.
EXT. PCI -- SAME DAY
A small, nondescript commercial building, "Paranormal Consultants Inc" stenciled discreetly on the door.
I believe all paranormal mysteries
have a scientific explanation...
INT. PCI -- CONTINUOUS A combination office/lab.
COLLIN CLARK in the lab area. 20's, white, geeky, chip on his shoulder. He's repairing a homemade device called THE M-PAC. It looks like a cross between an assault pistol and an ionic blow dryer.
...and technology will be the key to unlocking them.
Angle on ATHENA ORTIZ; Latina, 20's, street edge, hot but not on purpose. She's at the reception desk reading a book.
Some things will always be mysteries, Collin. (MORE)
The most you can do is harness their energy and use them for good.
(scoffs) Is that like the Witch's Hippocratic Oath?
I'm a bruja.
My mistake. A bruja.
Runs in the family. My grandmother's a bruja too. In the barrio she's famous. Oh really?
Yes, really. I've seen her do amazing things and she doesn't need any technology made from stuff you can get at Radio Shack.
Stung, Collin starts to retort but Josh enters with a BAG OF DONUTS.
Good morning.
Morning, Josh.
(off the bag) Apple fritter?

Maybe some other time, like when I'm skinny and have a dental plan. (then) Messages. A Mr. Markett called. He thinks his wife was a victim of spontaneous combustion and he'd like you to investigate and The New Age Society wants you to submit a paper on The Taos Hum Phenomenon.
Screen Mr. Markett, see if he really had a wife and I don't have time to submit a paper.
We also have that consultation with Jim Crowley, the dairy farmer? We should leave soon.
Josh crosses to Collin.
Apple fritter?
The new girl is not working out.
I think she's doing great.
She claims to be a witch, a bruja no less.
I know, Collin, and could we keep an open mind?
New angle. The phone RINGS, Athena answers.
PCI. (then) Josh, it's Barbara.
I'll take it in my office.
INT. JOSH'S OFFICE/PCI -- SAME
A cramped jumble of files, tapes, books, maps and photos, Josh picking up the phone.
Colonial, God, that's so eighties..is that olive? more like a moss... Honey? No, it's
What are we talking about?
INT. KITCHEN/JOSH'S HOUSE -- CONTINUOUS

BARBARA, Josh's wife; 30'S, attractive, suburban prim. She's looking out of the window, watching the house next door. A delivery truck is in the driveway, workmen toting a new living room set (the subject of her comments) into the house. TED STROUD and his wife, LASANDRA, supervising. They're attractive, black, 30's.
Ted and LaSandra, our new neighbors? I invited them over for lunch tomorrow. They seem nice enough.
Barbara waves at the Strouds, they wave back. As the dialogue continues Barb takes dishes out of the dishwasher and wipes off spots only she can see.
Is that why you called?
I just got off the phone with the Vice Principal.
school. She did?
It's the new boyfriend. Cal. taking advantage of her.
He's Athena appears in Josh's doorway, gestures, let's go.
I've got to go. We'll talk about it later.
They hang up but we stay with Barbara in the kitchen. ADAM enters. 7 years old, a smart kid trying to figure out how the world works.
I finished cleaning my room, Mom. Really?
Why don't we have a look?
INT. ADAM'S ROOM/JOSH'S HOUSE -- MOMENTS LATER
The room is very clean but Barbara is inspecting with the scrutiny of a Marine sergeant, Adam watching, tense.
Good job, Adam. (then) Don't you just feel better when everything is the way it's supposed to be? Sure.
I guess.
Barbara notices the bookshelf.
Adam, didn't we say you would alphabetize your books?
I don't think so.
Barbara and Adam examining a 20 PAGE, HANDWRITTEN CONTRACT titled "MY CONTRACT WITH MOM BY ADAM KEELING".
Here, Section B, item 7a. (reads) "I will put my books away in
alphabetical order."
But they look neat and everything.
You agreed to the contract, Adam. See? There's your signature.
But you said I had to sign it or I wouldn't get my allowance.
Give and take, sweetheart. We negotiated.
On Adam, confused.
INT. CAMRY -- SAME DAY Rural road.
Josh driving, Athena riding shotgun. Through the rear window, we can see Collin following them in a PANEL VAN.
I'm not sure if Athena's a bruja or not but I liked what she said in her interview.
INT. (FLASHBACK) JOSH'S OFFICE/PCI -- DAY
Athena interviewing with Josh.
My whole life I knew I was special but I didn't know how or why.
EXT. (FLASHBACK) STREET -- NIGHT
The barrio. Athena hanging with her rowdy HOME GIRLS, drinking and laughing.
And I was cool with that. Lots of people are ordinary, right?
INT. (FLASHBACK) LIVING ROOM/GRANDMOTHER'S HOUSE -- DAY
Athena's GRANDMOTHER, gripping Athena by the shoulders, imploring her, impassioned, her eyes ablaze.
But one day my grandmother called me over to her house and told me that I was a bruja like her and that if I didn't nurture my gift I would lose it.
INT. (FLASHBACK) JOSH'S OFFICE -- DAY
Josh and Athena, the interview continuing.
She said other people could waste their time but I didn't have the right. (beat) I had a calling. RESUME SCENE
Josh and Athena still driving.
I'm surprised this dairy farmer didn't just put the cow down.
He could have, but what if the rest of his herd had the same problem?
So he calls a paranormal consultant
before he calls a vet?
A vet might report the cow's symptoms to the Department of Agriculture. They could quarantine his farm.
Rock and a hard place.
Like a lot of our clients. They have a problem they can't go public with so they go online, look for help. Eventually they find us.
Speaking of somebody who needs help, what's up with Collin?
INT. PCI VAN -- CONTINUOUS
Collin driving, brooding.
Collin was a grad student in physics, top of his class.
INT. (FLASHBACK) DORM ROOM -- NIGHT
A manic Collin working at his laptop, surrounded by stacks of textbooks, complicated charts and diagrams.
(V.O.) He wrote his thesis on a variation of the big bang theory that included a race of cybernetic aliens.
INT. (FLASHBACK) OFFICE/UNIVERSITY -- DAY
Collin yelling at his PROFESSOR, then ripping a page out of his THESIS and eating it.
When his professor demanded revisions, Collin refused, saying he'd eat his thesis before he changed a word of it. RESUME SCENE
He was booted out of school, humiliating for a guy like him. (beat) He's got something to prove.
Up ahead, they can see the dairy farm. Jim and Ellen waving.
I think we're here.
EXT. DAIRY FARM -- MINUTES LATER
Josh, Athena and Collin meeting Jim and Ellen.
Mr. Keeling? I'm Jim Crowley and this is my wife, Ellen.
Please call me Josh. These are my associates, Athena Ortiz and Collin Clark. Everybody shakes hands.
We hear Delila BELLOW.
That's Delila.
We need to take a look at her.
Ellen notices Collin adjusting the M-PAC.
You're not going to kill her, are you?
Oh no, ma'am. This is an M-PAC device. Multi-Purpose Attack and
Control. It's charged with a nonlethal pulse of super-compressed argon gas.
Actually, I thought we'd let Athena take the lead.
Collin is put-out. Athena reacts, who me?
INT. MILKING BARN -- SAME DAY
The barn is a mess and full of shadows. Josh and Athena enter. They can hear Delila's HEAVY BREATHING coming from somewhere in the darkness. Warily, they creep forward.
Josh, I have a confession to make. Now?
I'm a little worried about the bruja thing. I mean I've watched my Grandmother a lot but I've never actually done stuff on my own.
We hear a threatening HUFF. Delila steps into view. Her condition has worsened, she looks truly berserk.
I think you better get started.
I'll try an incantation from El Salmo de Los Muertos. It's supposed to have a calming effect on animals.
Athena holds her hands out, palms up, intones in Spanish:
Este en la paz apacible tranquilo, ningun dano vendra a usted, este en la paz con mi y curare su dolor. Deje la energia de mi terraplen del
corazon usted con amor y deje el-Suddenly, Delila lets out a roaring BELLOW.
I don't think this is working.
Delila paws the ground and charges. Josh?
Josh seems unconcerned, Delila building speed. Josh?!
Anytime, Collin.

Collin appears with the M-PAC and fires. An INVISIBLE PULSE warps the air, hits Delila and stops her cold. She staggers. Angle on Josh, Athena and Collin as we hear Delila SLUMP to the ground. Collin looks at Athena, smug.
Radio Shack. There is no substitute.
A LITTLE LATER
Josh examining the unconscious Delila, Athena taking pictures with a digital camera, Jim and Ellen watching.
She's got no obvious contusions or lacerations, no blood anywhere.
Josh uses a mini-lite to check Delila's eyes.
If you don't mind my asking, how does a fella get into a field like this?
Started early. I was that kid. (off Delila) Her pupils are dilated but she's not aphasic. That kid?
INT. (FLASHBACK) YOUNG JOSH'S ROOM/APARTMENT -- EVENING

A chemistry set, models of spaceships hanging on wires, terrariums full of lizards and spiders, a poster of Stephen King looming over everything. YOUNG JOSH, 10 years old, reading a comic book with great concentration, stacks of them nearby, a PET SNAKE slithering up his arm.
You know, the loner, the oddball, the one everybody called "weirdo"? RESUME SCENE
Josh checks Delila's nose and ears.
I was a curious kid too, almost to the point of stupidity. (off Delila) Ears are clean, no discharge or ectoplastic residue.
EXT. (FLASHBACK) COAL MINE -- DUSK
Young Josh crawling out of an old coal mine, blackened with soot, sputtering, BATS flying past him.
If somebody said Big Foot was living in the old abandoned mine? I'd crawl in there and take a look.
RESUME SCENE Josh puts a THERMOMETER in Delila's mouth.
When I was ten years old I even camped out in the cemetery on Halloween night. I wanted to see a ghost.
How did your folks feel about that?
INT. YOUNG JOSH'S ROOM/APARTMENT -- SAME EVENING
Josh still reading his comic book.
My Mom raised me by herself and she worked nights.
Josh's mom, RACHEL enters. Attractive and warm, early thirties, nurses uniform. She's putting on her coat.
Are you sure you don't want to go trick or treating? I'm sure.
RESUME SCENE
She never knew and I never told her.
Josh checks Delila's thermometer.
Low grade fever, her tongue is discolored too. I'll need a blood sample.
And did you? See a ghost?
Josh, over here!
I saw a lot of things that night MOMENTS LATER
Everyone gathered around Collin, kneeling in the wreckage of Delila's stall, a MOUND OF MANURE in front of him. He shines a UV light on the manure illuminating HUNDREDS OF TINY WORMS. Worms.
Cows get worms all the time and they don't act like Delila.
I found these in Delila's stall and nowhere else.
I want them collected, Collin. Every single one.
Delila stirs, groans.
She's going to wake up pretty soon. Can we put her someplace secure?
I've got a breeding corral. We could roll her on to a tarp, drag her in there with the tractor.
EXT. MILKING BARN -- SAME DAY
The group comes out.
Do you think she's possessed?
She's showing some of the signs; anomalous aggression, preternatural strength -- but the worms, I'm not sure how they fit in. (then) You've got to stop selling milk.
Okay, but I can't do it forever. I've got a small operation here.
We'll work as fast as we can and I'll call you as soon as we know something.
Jim and Ellen move off.
I'll do a blood panel and full workup on the worms.
How come Delila is the only one that got them?
Gut feeling? She was deliberately infected. (to Collin) Make up a separate worm sample for me. I'll get started at home, see if I can identify them.
You sure you want to take those things into your house?
(patronizing) Worms are coldblooded. On ice they'll be inert.
INT. CAMRY -- THAT EVENING
Josh driving, A LITTLE RED COOLER on the seat beside him.
Spending Halloween night at the cemetery was one of those things, it seemed like a good idea at the time.
INT. (FLASHBACK) LIVING ROOM/APARTMENT -- NIGHT
Young Josh seeing Rachel to the door.
You can still change your mind. Mrs. Webberly will take you trick or treating with her kids.
No thanks. I'll be okay.
RESUME SCENE
I'll always wonder how my life would have been different if I'd gone trick or treating with Mrs. Webberly.
Josh arrives at his house, turns in the driveway.
INT. LIVING ROOM/STROUDS HOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
Angle on a SHOULDER HOLSTER WITH A GUN IT, hanging over a chair, an LAPD DETECTIVE'S GOLD SHIELD CLIPPED TO THE HOLSTER.
I had a conversation with Bob Fletcher, insurance guy, lives three houses down?
New angle: LaSandra moving her new sofa. Ted at the window, watching Josh get out of the car.
He told me Josh calls himself a paranormal consultant. Whatever that is.
Really? His wife told me he was a scientist. (beat) Are you going to help me move this sofa?
(helping her) I worked a long time so I could afford this house, so I could get away from noise and pollution and the traffic and most of all? The nutcases. Ted --
So here I am, in my dream house, mortgaged to the hilt and who is my next door neighbor? A nutcase!
You don't know that, Ted. You don't know that at all. Now we're going over there for lunch tomorrow and you're going to behave yourself, you understand?
INT. KITCHEN/JOSH'S HOUSE -- MOMENTS LATER
Josh crosses, puts the red cooler in the fridge.
You'd think it would be a problem, coming home after a day at work, making the transition from the strange and supernatural to the
mundane routines of normal life. Adam appears.
Mom and I have a disagreement over certain implied terms and exclusionary clauses. You may be asked to mediate.
Actually, I have trouble telling the difference.
INT. DINING ROOM/JOSH'S HOUSE -- SAME NIGHT
Josh, Barbara and Adam having dinner.
We have a clean slate with Ted and LaSandra and I'd like to keep it that way. As far as they're concerned we're a perfectly normal family.
You mean we're not?
Nobody else in the neighborhood seems to think so.
If you'd just let me talk about my work it wouldn't be such a big mystery.
There's only so much people can accept, Josh. It's one thing to say you believe in UFO's, it's another thing to say you've been on one.
DANNI enters. 16 years old, slovenly, not quite cute, a
Hey, kiddo. Hey.
Danni sits down, starts eating. Barbara shoots a look at Josh, then:
You skipped school, Danni?
Everybody skips school, Dad. like, what you do?
Didn't your contract stipulate penalties?
Don't tell me you signed that stupid thing.
(to Josh) Cal made her skip school.
Cal didn't make me do anything.
(to Danni) Mom said I had to sign it or I wouldn't get my allowance.
We negotiated, sweetheart. give and take.
So why'd you skip, Danni?
Cal's band was rehearsing and he needed me to like, do stuff.
He treats you like a maid servant.
He does not!
(to Josh) She cleans his house, which by the way, is down by the landfill.
Okay, Mom, we get it. You hate my boyfriend, just like you hate everything else about me.
(hurt) Danni!
They hear a car ROAR up to the house, the horn HONK.
That's Cal. I have to go.
I think I need to talk to him.
Danni groans.
EXT. JOSH'S HOUSE -- SAME
Josh and Danni come out of the house, cross to the driveway.
(whispers) He's the first cool boyfriend I've ever had. Don't embarrass me!
Don't embarrass her, like I'm the freak here.
They approach a tricked-out ACURA, a guy named CAL in the driver's seat. 18, unshaven, unwashed, tats, thinks a lot of himself. Danni gets in. Josh moves up to the driver's window.
Hey, Cal, how are you? Cal shrugs, too cool.
Listen, we're a little concerned about Danni skipping school.
Cal REVS the engine, drowning out Josh's voice.
It's really important that she keep
up her attendance -Cal keeps REVVING the engine, Josh trying to talk over it until finally:
We gotta go, Dad!
Cal gives Josh a fuck-you sneer and peels out of the driveway. Josh watches after the car, confounded.
People ask me all the time. Have the aliens landed? Are they living among us disguised as human beings? (beat) Yes, they are.
INT. BEDROOM/JOSH'S HOUSE -- THAT NIGHT
Josh in bed. Barbara at the dressing table, moisturizing.
What are we going to do? let her keep seeing him. We can't
Telling her not to will only make it worse.
You mean we can't do anything? have no control at all?
Speaking of control, don't you think your contract with Adam is a little much?
No, he needs clear-cut boundaries.
And what happens when you're not around to threaten him with lawsuits?
Barbara gets into bed.
That was exactly your attitude with Danni. Just let her do whatever she wants. Never mind that she doesn't care about her appearance or her grades or...herself.
She puts her head on Josh's chest, sighs a parents' sigh.
How did that happen?
I don't know.
Josh kisses her, then turns out the light.
It's human nature to try and control your life and the people you love. The mistake is thinking you had any in the first place.

We hold here in the darkness. Faintly at first, we hear an odd CRUNCHING SOUND. The camera moves off Josh and Barbara, following the sound out of the bedroom and through the house, the crunching getting louder and louder. Now we're in the kitchen heading towards the fridge, pushing through the door to the little red cooler, then into the cooler itself to a CLEAR PLASTIC PETRI DISH, nestled in ice, some WORMS inside. SNAP ZOOM -- THE WORMS They're eating through the plastic. END ACT 1
ACT 2 EXT. DAIRY FARM -- DAY
Delila in small metal corral. She's bellowing angrily, ramming the gate with her head. Jim and Ellen watching.
She's getting worse, Jim.
What the hell are those PCI people doing?!
INT. STUDY/JOSH'S HOUSE -- SAME DAY
Josh on the phone with Collin. On his computer screen is a PHOTO OF A WORM that looks just like Delila's worms.
Delila's worms are just your run-ofthe-mill coccidia. No reason they should be causing anything worse than a stomachache.
INT. PCI -- CONTINUOUS
Collin on the speakerphone with Josh.
We've got a bigger problem. Jim Crowley called. Delila is deteriorating fast.
How did her blood panel come back?
Negative. It's not an infection, drugs or toxicity.
Which leads us back to the worms.
(grudging) Athena has a theory.
Athena appears alongside Collin. She has a large, antique BOOK; the leather cover crumbling, pages yellow with age.
I had a chat with my Grandmother, Josh. (MORE)
She said the worms reminded her of something she heard about, something to do with an Indian tribe called the Michaguas. I found them in one of her books.
As Athena continues we FLASH TO PRIMITIVE DRAWINGS in the book appropriate to her narration.
The Michaguas lived in Peru about the time Columbus sailed for America. They used insects in their religious ceremonies; hornets, scorpions, army ants -- and worms.
Interesting.

The high priests extracted the worms from the stomachs of their most ferocious bulls and then performed a blood rite, basically, black magic, which transformed the worms into pequenos demonios, or little demons.
Oh please -ATHENA The worms were fed to the dairy cattle and then their milk was given to the tribe's warriors just before a big battle. It made them super-aggressive.
And Delila is acting like a superaggressive bull. (then) What happened to the dairy cattle?
The worms made them so crazy the priests eventually had to kill them.
Doesn't bode well for Delila.
Okay, let's suppose Delila's worms are somehow related to your ferocious-bull-black-magic-demonworms. How does that help us? Jim said he might have to put Delila down.
That's a point. (beat) So let's skip the diagnostics for now and find a way to kill the worms. Try commercial de-wormers first. I'm on it.
They hang up. We stay with Josh, thinking...
EXT. (FLASHBACK) LIVING ROOM/APARTMENT -- SAME EVENING
Young Josh looking out of the window. Below, he can see Rachel emerge from the building, clusters of KIDS in Halloween costumes moving past her. She stops, looks up at him and waves. He waves back.
I'll always remember my Mom's face that night. In a way, it was the last time I'd see it.
EXT.(FLASHBACK) APARTMENT BUILDING -- A MINUTE LATER
Young Josh comes out of the building wearing his backpack. He looks to see that Rachel is really gone, then runs off in the other direction.
INT. KITCHEN/JOSH'S HOUSE -- SAME DAY
Back to the present. Barbara arranging potato chips on a platter in a precise pattern, Adam bringing a bowl of creamy SOUR CREAM DIP out of the fridge. We catch a glimpse of the red cooler.
I'll tell you what, if you help me serve snacks to Ted and LaSandra? We'll forego 14B and 14C and use the ad-interim clause to credit you with compliance. I accept.
ADAM
Barbara takes the bowl of dip from Adam and sets it in the center of the platter.
There, that's perfect.
We hear Cal's Acura ROAR up to the house and then the HORN honk. Barbara stiffens, can't stand that sound.
INT. HALLWAY/JOSH'S HOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
Danni comes out of her room, responding to the HORN. As she starts down the hall, Josh comes out of the study. They stop, look at each other, Josh perplexed, Danni defensive.
You could do so much better, Danni.
Actually, Dad, I can't.
Your Mom is right about him. a jerk.
He's
Mom doesn't care. She just doesn't want me to embarrass her.
That's not true at all.
The phone in Josh's study RINGS.
I have to get that. So get it.
She walks past him and exits.
EXT. JOSH'S HOUSE-- SAME DAY
Cal sitting in the Acura, HONKING the horn. A HAND REACHES INTO FRAME and takes the key out of the ignition. New angle: Barbara, standing at the driver's side window, keys in hand. Cal sneers.
What's up, Barb?
Call me Mrs. Keeling and if you're going to pick up my daughter the least you could do is knock on the
door.
I'm not really into knocking.
And from now on you will not bring Danni home after her curfew and you will not make her skip school.
Yeah, well, here's the thing, Barb. (beat) Danni's s my bitch now and there's nothing you can do about it.
Barbara's jaw drops.
in the car.
Cal takes his keys back as Danni gets
Let's go already.
Cal cackles and peels out of the driveway.
INT. KITCHEN/JOSH'S HOUSE -- MOMENTS LATER
Barbara enters, furious, beside herself.
Danni's my bitch?! bitch?!!
She tries to calm down but can't. Impulsively, she grabs a chip off the perfect platter, takes a big scoop of sour cream dip and shoves it in her mouth.
INT. PCI -- SAME DAY
Collin working with chemicals, muttering to himself. Collin?
ATHENA (O.S.)
Angle on Athena looking into the industrial fridge. She holds up two empty PETRI DISHES, tiny holes chewed through them.
What happened to the worms?
INT. STUDY/JOSH'S HOUSE -- SAME DAY
Josh on the phone.
What do you mean the worms escaped?
INT. PCI -- CONTINUOUS
Collin and Athena on the speakerphone with Josh.
I mean they chewed through the polystyrene petri dishes.
FLASHBACK -- A SERIES OF SHOTS
We're inside the industrial fridge, the worms squiggling out of holes they've made in the petri dishes. They crawl on to the fridge rack...
Their reaction to the cold was to seek a warmer temperature but once they escaped the dishes they still had to get out of the fridge. The worms traveling along the bars of the rack. They're so
tiny the distances from their perspective are enormous.
Long way to go if you're a worm.
Exactly. And that's why they decided to generate their own heat. How?
Sex, of course. But they needed the proper environment, the chemical equivalent of soft
lighting and a Luther VanDross CD.
..the worms stop, raise their heads, look around...
In their case it was free glucose, acid triglycerides, casein proteins, lactose and -Collin --
JOSH (V.O.)
looking into the open industrial fridge. A CARTON OF COTTAGE CHEESE, A BOX OF YOGURT AND A QUART OF MILK are all bubbling over with froth and worms.
Anything made with milk. Cottage cheese, yogurt, ice cream.
Josh, didn't you take some worms home with you? And weren't they in petri dishes? RESUME JOSH
Yeah, but the dishes were in a cooler. (realizes) Also made of polystyrene.
Just then, Josh catches a momentary glimpse of Barbara crossing the hall. She's walking backwards.
I'll call you back.
INT. KITCHEN/JOSH'S HOUSE -- MOMENTS LATER
Josh rushes in, starts to open the fridge but sees the platter of chips on the counter, chips missing, scoop marks in the sour cream dip. He takes a closer look. The dip is moving. Oh no!
The doorbell RINGS.
EXT. JOSH'S HOUSE -- A MOMENT LATER.
Ted and LaSandra are there, Ted with a bottle of wine.
Remember, behave yourself.
It's not me you need to worry about.
The door opens, Josh frazzled but covering.
Ted! LaSandra! Nice to see you! What's going on?
Barbara invited us for lunch?
(beat) Right, yes, lunch! (then) Why don't you guys go on in the living room and make yourselves comfortable. I'll see what's
keeping Barb.
INT. BEDROOM/JOSH'S HOUSE -- MOMENTS LATER
Josh enters, sees Barbara, sitting at the dressing table in her slip. She's unnervingly still.
Honey? (no response) Honey, are you okay?
